name: textile-loomhouse
summary: A weaver's workshop dominated by large floor looms, with spinning wheels, bolts of finished cloth, and dye vats in the back. Threads and lint in every corner.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: rectangle — looms line up along the long axis

accent.purpose: dye-stained zone in the back near vats

primary_palette.note: production loomhouse — journeymen at three looms
dyer-weaver — integrated color and cloth production

story_prompts: commission rush — all looms running, master checking tension on each
dye day — vats steaming, weavers wearing stained aprons
thread-shortage — looms idle, master arguing with a merchant
night's work — one loom lit by candles, a pattern no one's seen before

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: steady working light (0.45-0.5) — weavers need to see thread

anti_patterns: looms need walking space on multiple sides — don't crowd them
dye vats stay away from finished cloth — cloth stacks near the exit
don't mirror loom orientation — weavers face the warp, make that visible
